demographics: I am 20 years old. I am female. I am Black or African American. I have never been married. I had 2 years of college education. My family income is $22,500 to $24,999. I am an independent, close to Democrat. I am liberal. My religion is Christian. I attend religious services several times a year.

events.event_text: I was walking home from school one day when a police car pulled over and the officer accused me of fitting the description of a shoplifter who had just been reported. I was handcuffed and taken to the station, despite my protests and pleas that I was innocent. My mom had to bail me out and I missed my afternoon classes. It was terrifying and humbling, making me realize that some people just see me as a stereotype rather than an individual.
[Step 2 skipped: constraint could not be satisfied — Biological incoherence: child_or_family_member_addiction_incarceration_or_crisis requires age ≥ 30, got 15]

I decided to join the school's debate team, which was mostly made up of white students. I was the only Black girl in the club, and some of them were surprised at first, but I made it clear that my voice and opinions mattered just as much as anyone else's.
[Step 4 skipped: constraint could not be satisfied — Biological incoherence: child_or_close_family_member_comes_out_as_lgbtq requires age ≥ 30, got 18]

During my freshman year of college, I became the target of racial slurs and microaggressions from a roommate. My primarily White dorm felt like a hostile environment, but I found solace and support in the Black Student Union. They connected me with resources and empowered me to report the incidents to the school.

Last year, I decided to intern at a local non-profit focused onraxial justice, driven by my past experiences and desire to make a difference. The team was diverse and passionate, but we faced numerous challenges due to limited funding. Despite the setbacks, seeing the impact we made in our community filled me with a sense of purpose and pride.

compressed_summaries.summary: This person, at age 15, was wrongfully accused and detained by police due to racial profiling, an experience that left them terrified and humbled. Later, they joined their school's debate team, predominantly white, and asserted their voice as the sole Black girl member.

This person joined a predominantly white debate team as the only Black girl, asserting her voice's validity. In college, they faced racial slurs and microaggressions from a roommate, finding support in the Black Student Union. They interned at a racial justice non-profit, overcoming funding challenges to make a community impact.

persona_card: This 20-year-old Black Christian woman, shaped by racial profiling and microaggressions, holds a nuanced worldview. She is morally traditional yet economically progressive, advocating for redistribution and government intervention to address class disparities. With a strong belief in structural racism, she trusts institutions minimally, particularly the legal system, due to personal experiences. Despite her close-knit religious community, she maintains an open and welcoming attitude towards social outgroups, having found support in diverse communities during her activism in racial justice.
